clusters.title: Can a social media ban protect young users?
clusters.neutral_headline: Can a social media ban protect young users
bullet_summary: Sites including Instagram, YouTube and TikTok will become inaccessible for millions of children, the prime minister has announced
The prime minister announced that under-16s will be banned from social media by spring 2027
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online

items.title: Can a social media ban protect young users?
items.standfirst: The UK says it's banning access to social media for those under age 16.
items.content: The UK says it's banning access to social media for those under age 16.

items.title: Under-16s to be banned from TikTok, YouTube and other social media by next spring, Starmer says
items.standfirst: Sites including Instagram, YouTube and TikTok will become inaccessible for millions of children, the prime minister has announced.
items.content: Sites including Instagram, YouTube and TikTok will become inaccessible for millions of children, the prime minister has announced.

items.content: Under-16s to be banned from social media Keir Starmer announces.

items.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for children under 16
items.standfirst: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.
items.content: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.

items.title: 'Positive' or 'unnecessary'? UK teens on social media ban

items.content: The prime minister announced that under-16s will be banned from social media by spring 2027.

items.standfirst: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer announces a ban for under-16s from major social media platforms
items.content: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer announces a ban for under-16s from major social media platforms

items.title: How will social media ban work and when does it start?
items.standfirst: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.
items.content: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

items.title: Britain will ban under-16s from social media apps, including TikTok and YouTube
items.standfirst: The ban will apply to platforms including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. The move makes the U.K. part of a growing global movement to tighten online safety for children.
items.content: The ban will apply to platforms including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. The move makes the U.K. part of a growing global movement to tighten online safety for children.(Image credit: Carlos Jasso)

items.title: Britain unveils sweeping ban on social media for under-16s
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online.

clusters.title: California governor Gavin Newsom accuses Donald Trump of directing DoJ to investigate him and his wife – US politics live
clusters.neutral_headline: Gavin Newsom says Trump directed DoJ to investigate him and his wife
bullet_summary: And what we’ve said is, OK, let’s talk about how exactly we’re going to do that
Gavin Newsom said on Monday the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
Newsom says he and wife targeted because he is considering running for president in 2028; DOJ has not confirmed probe
Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, said the Justice Department is investigating he and his wife, Jennifer

items.title: California governor Gavin Newsom accuses Donald Trump of directing DoJ to investigate him and his wife – US politics live
items.standfirst: Newsom accuses president of ‘coming after me because I am considering running for president’; justice department has yet to comment on claimGavin Newsom says Trump directed DoJ to investigate him and his wifeSign up for the Breaking News US emailJD Vance also dodged the question when he was asked by CNBC who would be at the signing of the agreement on Friday.Without addressing who would be present for the US side, he said they “expect the negotiating team from the Iranian side is going to be the Speaker of the House [Mohammad Bagher] Ghalibaf, and also the foreign minister [Abbas] Araghchi, along with a number of security officials and people who represent the different constituencies within their country.”I think it’s a great day for the American people … our expectation is that the strait is going to be opened in a toll-free way for the long term.That’s the sort of thing that we’re going to figure out in these technical negotiations. There are a lot of very important details to figure out that we’re actually going to sit at the table and discuss together and figure out a path forward on these details.And what we’ve said is, OK, let’s talk about how exactly we’re going to do that.They want access to an unsanctioned economy. We’ve talked about, ‘OK, we’re open to that,’ but that would require a long-term commitment to the inspection and verification regime. Continue reading...
items.content: Newsom accuses president of ‘coming after me because I am considering running for president’; justice department has yet to comment on claimGavin Newsom says Trump directed DoJ to investigate him and his wifeSign up for the Breaking News US emailJD Vance also dodged the question when he was asked by CNBC who would be at the signing of the agreement on Friday.Without addressing who would be present for the US side, he said they “expect the negotiating team from the Iranian side is going to be the Speaker of the House [Mohammad Bagher] Ghalibaf, and also the foreign minister [Abbas] Araghchi, along with a number of security officials and people who represent the different constituencies within their country.”I think it’s a great day for the American people … our expectation is that the strait is going to be opened in a toll-free way for the long term.That’s the sort of thing that we’re going to figure out in these technical negotiations. There are a lot of very important details to figure out that we’re actually going to sit at the table and discuss together and figure out a path forward on these details.And what we’ve said is, OK, let’s talk about how exactly we’re going to do that.They want access to an unsanctioned economy. We’ve talked about, ‘OK, we’re open to that,’ but that would require a long-term commitment to the inspection and verification regime. Continue reading...

items.title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says Justice Department investigating him and his wife
items.standfirst: California Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom said on Monday the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife. Newsom accused President Trump of using the DOJ to target him because of his political ambitions.
items.content: California Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom said on Monday the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife. Newsom accused President Trump of using the DOJ to target him because of his political ambitions.

items.title: Gavin Newsom says Trump directed DoJ to investigate him and his wife
items.standfirst: California governor says Trump is ‘coming after me because I am considering running for president’Gavin Newsom said on Monday that Donald Trump directed the US Department of Justice (DoJ) to investigate him and his wife Jennifer Siebel Newsom.The California governor said in a video statement that federal agents had knocked on the doors of family friends and former employees in recent days as part of an effort to find a crime, demanding records and “abusing the grand jury process”. Continue reading...
items.content: California governor says Trump is ‘coming after me because I am considering running for president’Gavin Newsom said on Monday that Donald Trump directed the US Department of Justice (DoJ) to investigate him and his wife Jennifer Siebel Newsom.The California governor said in a video statement that federal agents had knocked on the doors of family friends and former employees in recent days as part of an effort to find a crime, demanding records and “abusing the grand jury process”. Continue reading...

items.content: California governor is considered a potential Democratic presidential candidate for 2028

items.title: California Governor Newsom says US Department of Justice investigating him
items.standfirst: Newsom says he and wife targeted because he is considering running for president in 2028; DOJ has not confirmed probe.
items.content: Newsom says he and wife targeted because he is considering running for president in 2028; DOJ has not confirmed probe.

items.title: California Governor Newsom says Trump ordered investigation into him and his wife
items.standfirst: California Governor Gavin Newsom, a Democrat and longtime political rival of Republican President Donald Trump, said Monday the president had ordered the US Justice Department to launch a politically motivated investigation into him and his wife.
items.content: California Governor Gavin Newsom, a Democrat and longtime political rival of Republican President Donald Trump, said Monday the president had ordered the US Justice Department to launch a politically motivated investigation into him and his wife.

items.content: California Gov. Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, said the Justice Department is investigating he and his wife, Jennifer.

clusters.title: Zelenskyy visits historic Kyiv cathedral after deadly Russian strike
clusters.neutral_headline: Zelenskyy visits historic Kyiv cathedral after deadly Russian strike
bullet_summary: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital
Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World and others

items.title: Zelenskyy visits historic Kyiv cathedral after deadly Russian strike
items.standfirst: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visited a landmark Kyiv cathedral damaged by Russian missile and drone strikes.
items.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy visited a landmark Kyiv cathedral damaged by Russian missile and drone strikes.

items.title: Ukraine: Kyiv's historic cathedral set ablaze in Russian attack
items.standfirst: One of #Ukraine's most important religious landmarks was hit in an overnight Russian attack on June 15. Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky visited the cathedral and called for more pressure on Russian President Vladimir #Putin ahead of his attendance at the #G7 summit in #Paris.
items.content: One of #Ukraine's most important religious landmarks was hit in an overnight Russian attack on June 15. Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky visited the cathedral and called for more pressure on Russian President Vladimir #Putin ahead of his attendance at the #G7 summit in #Paris.

items.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
items.standfirst: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.
items.content: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.

items.title: Deadly Russian attacks fuel blaze at centuries-old monastery in Kyiv
items.standfirst: Deadly Russian strikes across Ukraine overnight sparked an intense blaze at a 1,000-year-old monastery in Ukraine’s capital Kyiv, one of the country’s most important religious and cultural landmarks
items.content: Deadly Russian strikes across Ukraine overnight sparked an intense blaze at a 1,000-year-old monastery in Ukraine’s capital Kyiv, one of the country’s most important religious and cultural landmarks

items.title: Fire engulfs centuries-old Ukrainian monastery after deadly strikes
items.standfirst: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital. Moscow denied responsibility for the attack but President Zelenskyy described it as “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture.”
items.content: Fire engulfed the Kyiv Perchersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage site founded in 1051, after a massive Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine’s capital. Moscow denied responsibility for the attack but President Zelenskyy described it as “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture.”

items.title: US Air Force B-52 bomber plane crashes after take off in California
items.standfirst: Officials did not say if anyone was injured in the crash, but the aircraft typically carry a crew of five.
items.content: Officials did not say if anyone was injured in the crash, but the aircraft typically carry a crew of five.

items.standfirst: An Air Force B-52 crashed shortly after takeoff at California's Edwards Air Force Base on Monday, the base said. Emergency crews are responding.
items.content: An Air Force B-52 crashed shortly after takeoff at California's Edwards Air Force Base on Monday, the base said. Emergency crews are responding.

items.title: US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base

items.content: It was not immediately clear if there were any casualties in crash at base north of Los Angeles, California.

items.content: Cape Verde stuns Spain with a goalless draw inspired by a 40-year-old goalkeeper who has gone viral on social media.

items.title: Vozinha heroics help Cape Verde hold Spain to goalless draw
items.standfirst: European champions Spain were held to surprise 0-0 draw by debutants Cape Verde in their World Cup opener, with 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha producing a standout performance for his side in their Group H clash.
items.content: European champions Spain were held to surprise 0-0 draw by debutants Cape Verde in their World Cup opener, with 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha producing a standout performance for his side in their Group H clash.

items.title: Spain held to shock draw by Cape Verde in their World Cup opener
items.standfirst: Lamine Yamal comes off the bench but cannot help Spain overcome World Cup debutants Cape Verde in 0-0 draw.
items.content: Lamine Yamal comes off the bench but cannot help Spain overcome World Cup debutants Cape Verde in 0-0 draw.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Debutants Cape Verde hold European champions Spain to shock draw
items.standfirst: World Cup newcomers Cape Verde held off favourites Spain in a goalless draw in Atlanta on Monday, delivering a defensive masterclass in their first appearance on football's biggest stage to produce the first shock of the tournament against the European champions.
items.content: World Cup newcomers Cape Verde held off favourites Spain in a goalless draw in Atlanta on Monday, delivering a defensive masterclass in their first appearance on football's biggest stage to produce the first shock of the tournament against the European champions.

clusters.title: Ban on Palestine Action was lawful, court of appeal rules
clusters.neutral_headline: Ban on Palestine Action was lawful, court of appeal rules
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Ban on Palestine Action was lawful, court of appeal rules
items.standfirst: Judges overturn decision of high court that government proscription of group under Terrorism Act was wrongThe home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled.A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrong. Continue reading...
items.content: Judges overturn decision of high court that government proscription of group under Terrorism Act was wrongThe home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled.A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrong. Continue reading...

items.title: Palestine Action ban is lawful, Court of Appeal rules
items.standfirst: The Home Office had challenged a High Court ruling that the group's proscription should be quashed.
items.content: The Home Office had challenged a High Court ruling that the group's proscription should be quashed.

items.title: Son of Norway’s crown princess convicted of rape and sentenced to four years in prison
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences including two counts of rape.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning in a courthouse packed with journalists, nearly three months after Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences including two counts of rape.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning in a courthouse packed with journalists, nearly three months after Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
clusters.neutral_headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Reuters Top News

items.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
items.standfirst: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.
items.content: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

items.title: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at ten million
items.standfirst: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
items.content: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
